# UC-22.10.34 · Break-Glass — Emergency Access Reason Codes & Post-Review

## Description

Ensures every break-glass chart override includes a reason code and is followed by supervisor attestation within policy timelines.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for HIPAA Security compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes epic, itsm and sourcetypes epic:breakglass, epic:breakglass`, snow:incident. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Map `session_id` join keys to your ticketing design; (2) 24h alert on missing reason; (3) 72h alert on missing attestation; (4) quarterly privacy committee rollup. Indexes required: `index=epic`, `index=itsm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=epic:breakglass`, `sourcetype=epic:breakglass``, `sourcetype=snow:incident`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk DB Connect (2686).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:breakglass` (`USER_ID`, `PAT_ID`, `reason_code`, `session_id`), `index=itsm` ServiceNow tasks (`short_description`, `parent_incident`).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Map `session_id` join keys to your ticketing design; (2) 24h alert on missing reason; (3) 72h alert on missing attestation; (4) quarterly privacy committee rollup.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=epic sourcetype="epic:breakglass" earliest=-30d
| eval has_reason=if(isnotnull(reason_code) AND reason_code!="","OK","MISSING")
| join type=left session_id [
    search index=itsm sourcetype="snow:incident" earliest=-30d short_description="*break*glass*"
    | rename number as attestation_ticket, sys_id as session_id ]
| eval reviewed=if(isnotnull(attestation_ticket),"Yes","No")
| where has_reason!="OK" OR reviewed="No"
| table _time, USER_ID, PAT_ID, reason_code, has_reason, reviewed
```

Understanding this SPL

**Break-Glass — Emergency Access Reason Codes & Post-Review (§164.502(a)(2)(ii) / policy)** — Ensures every break-glass chart override includes a reason code and is followed by supervisor attestation within policy timelines.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:breakglass` (`USER_ID`, `PAT_ID`, `reason_code`, `session_id`), `index=itsm` ServiceNow tasks (`short_description`, `parent_incident`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk DB Connect (2686). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: epic; **sourcetype**: epic:breakglass. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=epic, sourcetype="epic:breakglass", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **has_reason** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **reviewed** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where has_reason!="OK" OR reviewed="No"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: _time, PAT_ID, USER_ID, attestation_ticket, has_reason, reason_code, reviewed.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the EHR audit trail (Epic, Cerner), walk known-good cases from the same workflow class, and confirm index coverage, field extractions, and access permissions match expectations.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline (open attestations), Table (violations), Single value (% reviewed).

## SPL

```spl
index=epic sourcetype="epic:breakglass" earliest=-30d
| eval has_reason=if(isnotnull(reason_code) AND reason_code!="","OK","MISSING")
| join type=left session_id [
    search index=itsm sourcetype="snow:incident" earliest=-30d short_description="*break*glass*"
    | rename number as attestation_ticket, sys_id as session_id ]
| eval reviewed=if(isnotnull(attestation_ticket),"Yes","No")
| where has_reason!="OK" OR reviewed="No"
| table _time, USER_ID, PAT_ID, reason_code, has_reason, reviewed
```

## Visualization

Timeline (open attestations), Table (violations), Single value (% reviewed).
